team_context.competitive_window: competing — but in transition. 9-8 in 2025 after 14-3 in 2024, missed playoffs. GM Kwesi Adofo-Mensah fired in January 2026. Still believe they can compete with the right QB play from J.J. McCarthy or potential addition of Kyler Murray.
team_context.cap_situation: very_tight — started offseason ~$45M over the cap. Have restructured Jefferson, Darrisaw, Murphy, Hockenson contracts and released Allen/Hargrave to get compliant. Currently ~$26M in space after all moves but need to fund draft picks (~$14M). Little room for big signings.
team_context.trade_posture: Sellers at select positions due to cap pressure. Actively shopping Jonathan Greenard (Eagles have called, Day 2 pick ask). Willing to listen on Jordan Addison given off-field concerns and looming 5th-year option decision. Will protect core of Jefferson, Darrisaw, McCarthy. Looking to build around young talent and draft picks after big free agency spending backfired in 2025.

players.name: J.J. McCarthy
players.performance_evidence: First-year starter in 2025, went 9-8. Missed entire rookie year with meniscus surgery. 1,632 passing yards in limited action (missed games to concussion, hand fracture). Showed improvement late in season but inconsistent overall. Beat reporters note he 'looked much better at the end of the season' but mostly against weaker opponents. Still developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Former top-10 pick on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years left. Upside is massive — 23 years old, strong arm, in Kevin O'Connell's system. But hasn't proven he's a franchise QB yet. Worth a mid-to-late 1st due to age, contract value, and draft pedigree. Comparable to what developing QBs on rookie deals command.

players.availability_reasoning: Vikings are pursuing Kyler Murray and have been linked to veteran QBs all offseason. If Murray signs, McCarthy's future becomes unclear — he could be traded if the right offer comes. But Minnesota may also keep him as competition. Premium price required — they wouldn't move a former 1.10 cheaply.
players.injury_notes: Missed entire 2024 rookie season with meniscus surgery. Suffered concussion in 2025 Week 11 vs Packers, then hairline fracture in right hand later in season. Durability is a legitimate concern.
players.trade_buzz: Significant. Vikings are meeting with Kyler Murray. Multiple reports linking them to veteran QBs. If they sign Murray, McCarthy becomes available. Cameron Wolfe reported mutual interest between Colts and Vikings on Anthony Richardson swap.

players.name: Max Brosmer
players.performance_evidence: Got his first NFL start in 2025 at Seattle — threw 4 INTs with a 32.8 passer rating in a shutout loss. Also filled in for parts of other games when McCarthy was hurt. Clearly not a viable starter at this point.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimum-salary backup QB with no evidence of starter-level play. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: If Vikings sign Murray and keep McCarthy, Brosmer becomes expendable. Could be moved for a conditional late pick or simply cut.

players.name: Aaron Jones Sr.
players.performance_evidence: Missed time with hamstring injury in 2025. Still a solid locker room presence but production declining with age. Was set to be released before agreeing to a revised deal to lower base salary and stay for 2026. 32 years old entering the season.

trade_value.reasoning: 32-year-old RB on a reworked deal with declining production. Vikings themselves were planning to cut him — no team would give up meaningful draft capital. Worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Vikings were planning to release him before he agreed to a pay cut. At 32, he's a stopgap. If a draft pick RB emerges or another team has need, Minnesota would move him for any pick.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury caused him to miss a chunk of the 2025 season.
players.character_notes: Excellent locker room presence, widely respected.
players.trade_buzz: Was reportedly going to be released at start of league year. Agreed to restructured deal instead. No active trade market.

players.name: Jordan Mason
players.performance_evidence: Led Vikings in rushing yards (758) in 2025. Acquired from 49ers via trade in 2025 offseason. Assumed lead rusher role. Signed 2-year, $10.5M extension. Still young at 25 and productive when given carries.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old productive RB on a reasonable 1-year remaining deal. RB values are depressed league-wide. Worth a 4th-round pick to a team needing a starter, but positional value limits the return.

players.availability_reasoning: Vikings need Mason if Jones is limited/aging. He's their RB of the future and just 25. Would take a solid Day 3 pick to pry him away, and only if they have a replacement plan.

players.name: Justin Jefferson
players.performance_evidence: 84 catches for 1,048 yards and 2 TDs in 2025 — his numbers were suppressed by poor QB play and inconsistency under center. 2022 AP Offensive Player of the Year, 3x Pro Bowler, 1x All-Pro. Career 98.3 receiving yards per game is highest in NFL history. Highest-paid non-QB in NFL history at $35M AAV.

trade_value.reasoning: Effectively untradeable. Elite WR in his prime on a long-term deal, but with ~$72M in dead cap. Even if a team wanted to give multiple 1sts, the dead cap makes this impossible. Would be worth 3+ first-round picks if the contract allowed it.

players.availability_reasoning: Face of the franchise, massive dead cap (~$72M if traded), still elite. Vikings restructured his deal just days ago to create cap space — they are committed. The team let multiple teams know they had 'zero intention of trading Jefferson' when inquiries came.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury limited him in 2023 (10 games). Generally durable otherwise.

players.name: Jordan Addison
players.performance_evidence: Highly talented when on the field — showed flashes as WR2 beside Jefferson. But production inconsistent in 2025 due to 3-game suspension to start season, benching in London for missing walkthrough, and led team in drops. Spotrac projects 5th-year option at ~$17.5M for 2027. Would be WR1 on many teams.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old former 1st-round WR on final year of cheap rookie deal. Enormous talent but off-field red flags (DUI, trespassing, disciplinary issues) create risk discount. On a minimum-cost contract, he's a high-upside add for any team. Worth a 2nd-round pick — teams have called.

players.availability_reasoning: Vikings face a May 1 deadline on his 5th-year option (~$17.5M guaranteed). Beat reporters say the team could trade him rather than commit long-term given off-field concerns. Multiple teams have called. Jalen Nailor leaving to Raiders and Tai Felton's emergence gives Vikings options.
players.character_notes: DUI arrest in 2024 (suspended 3 games to start 2025). Driving 140 MPH in 2023. Trespassing incident after 2025 season. Benched for missing walkthrough in London. Pattern of immaturity is the primary concern.
players.trade_buzz: Significant. Multiple reports of teams calling. Patriots linked (Addison publicly dismissed them). Vikings may trade him before May 1 5th-year option deadline. Brad Berreman of The Viking Age wrote teams could 'fill their void at the position without having to make a multi-year commitment.'

players.name: Tai Felton
players.performance_evidence: 2025 4th-round pick who saw limited opportunities as a rookie behind Jefferson, Addison, and Nailor. Expected to step into WR3 role in 2026 with Nailor departing to Raiders. Still unproven but has upside.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven 2nd-year WR on a cheap rookie deal. Minimal track record but some developmental upside. Worth a late-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: With Nailor gone, Felton is the projected WR3. Vikings need him in 2026 and wouldn't move him cheaply. He'd only be traded if they got a clear overpay or drafted a replacement.

players.name: T.J. Hockenson
players.performance_evidence: Just 51 catches for 438 yards and 3 TDs in 2025 — significant decline from pre-ACL production. 2x Pro Bowler but hasn't been the same since tearing his ACL in Week 16 of 2023. Still graded well as a blocker per PFF. Restructured contract eliminates 2027, making him a FA after 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: 29-year-old declining TE on an expiring deal with a $15.6M cap hit and $10.5M dead cap. The dead cap makes a trade nearly impossible. If a team absorbed the contract, he'd be worth a 5th at best based on current production.

players.availability_reasoning: Vikings restructured rather than cutting him, but he's on a 1-year deal now. If a team offered a mid-round pick and was willing to absorb the contract, Minnesota would listen. High dead cap ($10.5M) makes this unlikely though.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in Week 16 of 2023 season. Has not returned to pre-injury form across 25 games since returning. Production has 'slowly dropped off.'
players.trade_buzz: Was a trade/cut candidate before restructuring. Beat reporters said he was unlikely to fetch trade interest even at lower cap hits.

players.name: Josh Oliver
players.performance_evidence: Extended with Vikings during 2025 season. Solid blocking TE and depth receiving option. Not a significant pass-catching threat but fills a role in the offense.

trade_value.reasoning: Backup TE on a modest deal. Minimal standalone trade value. Worth a late-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece who could be moved if Vikings draft a TE. Not being actively shopped but has a price.

players.name: Christian Darrisaw
players.performance_evidence: When healthy, one of the best young LTs in football. Extension in July 2024 made him highest-paid OT at the time. Missed time due to knee injury in 2025 — has never played a full 17-game season in his career. Restructured contract in March 2026 dropped cap hit to $13.5M but pushed cap hits to $27.9M, $34.1M, $31.5M in future years.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old franchise LT on a 4-year deal. When healthy, he's a top-10 tackle. The $43.8M dead cap makes him completely untradeable in practice. If he could be moved, he'd command multiple 1sts.

players.availability_reasoning: Just restructured his deal to help the team get cap compliant. $43.8M dead cap if traded. Franchise cornerstone LT at 26. Zero chance of being moved.
players.injury_notes: Has never played a full season. Knee injury in 2025, various issues in prior years. Durability is the main concern with an otherwise elite player.

players.name: Donovan Jackson
players.performance_evidence: 2025 1st-round pick out of Ohio State drafted specifically to upgrade the interior OL and protect J.J. McCarthy. Started as a rookie. Still developing but showed promise. The entire OL was plagued by injuries in 2025, limiting the unit's potential.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old 1st-round IOL on a rookie deal with 3 years left. Premium value due to age, contract, and draft pedigree. Still proving himself but the upside and cost control make him valuable. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: 2025 1st-round pick entering year 2. Vikings invested the 24th pick to protect their young QB. No chance they move him this early.

players.name: Michael Jurgens
players.performance_evidence: Backup center who steps into the starting role after Ryan Kelly's retirement following 3 concussions in 2025. UDFA who has been developing behind Kelly. Unproven as a full-time starter but is the projected C1 for 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven starting center on a cheap deal. Minimal trade value until he establishes himself. Worth a late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: If Vikings draft a center or sign one in free agency, Jurgens could be pushed down the depth chart. But as the projected starter, he's needed unless they find an upgrade.

players.name: Will Fries
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 5-year, $87.7M contract in 2025 free agency after strong play with the Colts. Looked like 'a shell of himself' recovering from injury in 2025 per beat writers. Still only 27 and expected to bounce back. Every projected starter on the OL except Fries missed time due to injuries in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old guard on a large but fair contract with 4 years left. High dead cap ($24M) makes a trade impractical. When healthy, solid starter. Worth a 3rd-round pick in a vacuum but dead cap prevents movement.

players.availability_reasoning: Recently signed to a big deal. $24M dead cap makes a trade extremely unlikely. Vikings are committed. Would need a massive overpay to consider moving him.
players.injury_notes: Dealt with injury in 2025 that affected his play. Expected healthy for 2026.

players.name: Brian O'Neill
players.performance_evidence: PFF grade of 80.8 in 2024, allowed just 2 sacks in 1,000+ snaps. Has been one of the NFL's highest-graded RTs for years. Spotrac market value at $21.1M AAV. Turns 31 in September but aging well — 'arguably the Vikings' single-most effective lineman for seven seasons.' Zero guaranteed money remaining.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber RT on an expiring deal with $23.2M cap hit but only $3.7M dead cap. A contending team that needs a RT could absorb the salary for a year of elite play. Worth a 2nd-round pick but the high salary limits the market. Extension with new team would be needed.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his deal with zero guarantees. Vikings are actively discussing an extension to lower his 2026 cap hit. If extension talks fail, he becomes a candidate to be traded rather than let walk for nothing. But the Vikings want to keep him — he's their best OL.
players.trade_buzz: Vikings have reportedly approached O'Neill about a contract extension. If talks stall, he could become available before the draft. Star Tribune noted the team 'could explore a contract extension' for him.

players.name: Levi Drake Rodriguez
players.performance_evidence: Former UDFA who has worked his way onto the roster. With Jonathan Allen and Javon Hargrave both released, Rodriguez steps into a starting role by default. Depth piece elevated due to roster turnover. Limited sample size of meaningful production.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven UDFA DL on a minimum deal. Minimal trade value. Roster filler who benefits from the departure of Allen and Hargrave.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece who could be replaced through the draft. Would be moved for any pick offered.

players.name: Jalen Redmond
players.performance_evidence: Minnesota's best defensive lineman in 2025, playing ~75% of defensive snaps. Former UFL player who emerged as a feel-good story — went from practice squad to arguably the Vikings' top interior DL. Tendered on a 1-year exclusive rights deal for 2026. Extension talks expected this offseason.

trade_value.reasoning: Breakout interior DL on a dirt-cheap 1-year tender. Only 27 years old. Excellent value for any team. Worth a 4th-round pick but the Vikings desperately need him with Allen and Hargrave gone — they can't afford to trade him.

players.availability_reasoning: Vikings just lost Allen and Hargrave from their DL. Redmond is essential — he was their best interior DL in 2025. Team is expected to pursue a multi-year extension. Only an overpay would pry him away.

players.name: Javon Hargrave
players.performance_evidence: Generated 31 pressures and 4 sacks in 16 games in 2025. Was released by Vikings on March 11, 2026 and signed a 2-year, $23M deal with the Green Bay Packers. No longer on the Vikings roster.

trade_value.reasoning: RELEASED. Hargrave was cut by the Vikings on March 11, 2026 and signed with the Packers. He should NOT appear on the active roster. This slot will likely be filled by a draft pick or free agent signing.

players.availability_reasoning: RELEASED from the Vikings. Already signed with Green Bay Packers. This depth chart slot is vacant.

players.name: Jonathan Greenard
players.performance_evidence: 12 sacks in 2024 earned Pro Bowl selection. Dropped to 3 sacks in 12 games in 2025 due to injury, but PFF ranked him 12th against the run and 18th in pass rush among qualifying EDGE defenders. 38 career sacks in 6 seasons. Finished 17th in QB hurries and 31st in total pressures despite missing 5 games. 4-year, $76M contract with zero remaining guarantees.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl EDGE rusher with 2 years of control at $19M/year — below market for elite edge rushers (Hendrickson just got $28M/yr, Phillips $30M/yr). No guarantees remaining gives acquiring team flexibility. Vikings seeking a Day 2 pick. Worth a 2nd-rounder to a contender who will extend him. Cap savings of $12.25M for Minnesota incentivizes the trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Adam Schefter reported 'Vikings are now open to trading Pro Bowl edge rusher Jonathan Greenard.' Greenard is seeking a raise, Vikings are cap-strapped. Eagles have been in active discussions including talking to his agent about an extension. Dallas Turner (2024 1st-round pick, 8 sacks in 2025) is ready to replace him. This is the most likely trade to happen on the Vikings roster.
players.injury_notes: Missed 5 games in 2025 with an undisclosed injury. When available, still productive.
players.trade_buzz: MAJOR. Eagles are the primary suitor per Dianna Russini of The Athletic. Schefter confirmed Vikings are open to trading him. Team is 'seeking a Day 2 pick.' Greenard wants a raise from his $19M salary. Multiple outlets confirm this is an active trade negotiation as of March 11, 2026.

players.name: Blake Cashman
players.performance_evidence: Central figure in Brian Flores' defense, wore the green dot (defensive play-caller helmet) before injury. Signed 3-year, $22.5M deal in 2024. Injured hamstring in season opener of 2025, lost starting job to Eric Wilson. Spotrac noted with cap trouble, 'there's a world where Minnesota flips out of this contract.'

trade_value.reasoning: Solid ILB on an expiring-ish deal with modest cap hit. Coming off injury and lost starting job. Worth a 4th-round pick to a team needing LB depth, but the Vikings may keep him for depth behind Wilson.

players.availability_reasoning: Lost starting job to Eric Wilson in 2025. Has 1 year left at $9.5M cap hit with $5.1M dead cap — not an easy cut. But Spotrac flagged him as a potential cap casualty. If a team offers a mid-round pick, Vikings would consider it.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring injury in 2025 season opener cost him significant time and his starting job.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac mentioned him as a candidate to flip for cap savings.

players.name: Eric Wilson
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season: 115 tackles, 17 TFLs, 4 forced fumbles, 6.5 sacks, 10 QB hits. NFL's 3rd-best pressure rate (15.3%) among LBs. Took over starting job from Cashman and thrived in Flores' blitz-heavy scheme. Just re-signed on a 3-year, $22.5M deal. 'The most impressive single season of his NFL career.'

trade_value.reasoning: 32-year-old LB coming off a career year on a fresh 3-year deal. Excellent value contract at $7.5M AAV for his production. But age (32) and the fact that 2025 could be an outlier limit the ceiling. Worth a late 3rd/early 4th.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed to a 3-year deal. Vikings clearly want him as a core piece of Flores' defense. Would take a significant offer to move him immediately after signing.

players.name: Andrew Van Ginkel
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl and 2nd-team All-Pro in 2024: career highs of 79 tackles, 18 TFLs, 11.5 sacks, 2 INTs returned for TDs. Extended with 1-year, $23M deal in April 2025. Production likely dipped in 2025 (specific stats not found) but remains a key piece of Flores' defense. Extremely versatile — drops into coverage ~20% of the time.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl EDGE on an expiring deal (1 year left) with $18M cap hit. High dead cap ($12M) limits trade feasibility. When healthy, elite production. But at 31 on an expensive final year, the return is capped. Worth a 2nd-round pick in theory but dead cap complications reduce the realistic return.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive piece in Flores' scheme. Just extended last year. High dead cap makes a trade difficult but not impossible. If Greenard is traded and Turner steps up, Van Ginkel becomes even more important. Vikings would need a significant overpay.

players.name: James Pierre
players.performance_evidence: Best season of his career in 2025 with Pittsburgh: 30 tackles, 11 pass deflections, 1 INT, 1 fumble recovery TD in 14 games (5 starts). PFF gave him an 88.9 coverage grade — 1st among defenders with 200+ coverage snaps. Allowed just 16 catches on 35 targets with 57.2 opponent passer rating. Just signed 2-year, $8.5M deal with Vikings.

trade_value.reasoning: 29-year-old CB coming off a career year on a cheap 2-year deal. But small sample size of starting experience (13 career starts in 6 years) limits value. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency so unlikely to be immediately traded. But with only $3.7M guaranteed and a low dead cap, he could be moved if the Vikings draft a CB early and have depth.

players.name: Byron Murphy Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl in 2024 with 6 INTs. Regressed in 2025: 71 tackles, 2 INTs, 7 PDs, but PFF ranked him just 73rd of 114 CBs. Signed 3-year, $54M deal in 2025 free agency. Restructured contract in March 2026 dropped cap hit from $19.6M to $8.3M but created $29.8M dead cap. Versatile — can play outside or slot.

trade_value.reasoning: 28-year-old CB with Pro Bowl pedigree on a 2-year deal at $18M AAV. Cheap cap hit in 2026 ($8.3M) but massive dead cap ($29.8M) makes him completely untradeable. If the dead cap didn't exist, he'd be worth a 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: $29.8M dead cap after restructure makes him unmovable. Vikings would lose money by trading him. He's locked in for 2026-2027.

players.name: Theo Jackson
players.performance_evidence: Defensive back who plays multiple spots in Flores' scheme. Listed at nickel but has also played safety. Depth piece who stepped into an increased role in 2025. Flores seemed to prefer Jay Ward over Jackson down the stretch of 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Low-cost rotational DB on a minimum deal. Minimal trade value. Depth piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth DB who could be replaced. Would be moved for any late-round pick.

players.name: Joshua Metellus
players.performance_evidence: Carved out an irreplaceable hybrid role in Flores' defense. Plays safety, nickel, and blitzes off the edge — Flores uses him as a chess piece. Beat writers described him as 'irreplaceable' and urged the team to extend him. Rushed the passer more than 6x the NFL average for DBs. Critical to Flores' high-blitz scheme.

trade_value.reasoning: Versatile safety/hybrid in his prime at 28. If on a multi-year deal, would be worth more. As a pending FA, his value is limited since a team could just sign him. Worth a late 3rd if under contract, but his contract status (likely UFA or tender) limits this.

players.availability_reasoning: Set to hit free agency (was listed among 2026 UFAs). If re-signed, he becomes a core piece. Vikings have been urged to extend him. His unique fit in Flores' scheme makes him more valuable to MIN than anyone else.

players.name: Harrison Smith
players.performance_evidence: 6x Pro Bowler, franchise legend. Career-low 54 tackles in 2025 but still earned 5th-best PFF defensive grade among Vikings. 10 PBUs, 2 INTs in 15 games. Released with post-June 1 designation on March 11, 2026 — hasn't decided on retirement yet. If he returns, would be on a veteran minimum deal. Still effective in Flores' scheme but physically declining at 37.

trade_value.reasoning: 37-year-old safety who has been released. If he returns, it would be on a minimum deal. Zero trade value — any team can sign him outright if he decides to play.

players.availability_reasoning: Released by Vikings on March 11, 2026 with post-June 1 designation. Has not decided on retirement vs. returning for a 15th season. If he plays, it would likely be back with Minnesota on a vet minimum. Not a trade candidate.
players.injury_notes: Personal health issue sidelined him for most of 2025 training camp. Missed first 2 games getting back in shape. At 37, durability is a constant concern.
players.character_notes: Franchise icon, locker room leader, team captain. If he returns, it's for the culture as much as the on-field contribution.

players.name: Will Reichard
players.performance_evidence: Named first or second-team All-Pro in each of his first two seasons. Set franchise record with 62-yard field goal in 2025. One of the NFL's top kickers on a dirt-cheap rookie deal. Exceptional value.

trade_value.reasoning: All-Pro kicker on a minimum-cost rookie deal. Kickers rarely get traded but this one is elite and cheap. Worth a 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: All-Pro kicker on a rookie deal. Zero reason to move him. Kickers aren't traded but even if they were, he's a franchise keeper.

players.name: Kyler Murray
players.performance_evidence: Murray was traded to Minnesota in the 2025 offseason after Arizona moved on. He's a former #1 overall pick and Pro Bowler (2021) with dual-threat ability — career 65% completion rate, 26 TDs per 16-game pace when healthy. His 2024 season with the Cardinals showed a return to strong play post-ACL (torn late 2022), throwing for ~3,500 yards and 21 TDs in a full season. Dynamic scrambler with 500+ rushing yards per year when healthy. However, he's coming off an inconsistent stretch and is now entering his age-29 season. Arm talent and mobility remain high-end but decision-making and pocket presence questions persist. Top 10-12 QB when at his best.

trade_value.reasoning: Starting-caliber franchise QB with 3 years remaining on his deal, dual-threat ability, and still in his athletic prime at 28-29. The massive cap hit ($42M+) and significant dead cap limit trade flexibility, but QB scarcity means a QB-needy team would pay a 1st-round equivalent. His ACL recovery and inconsistency prevent him from reaching elite QB trade value (top 5 pick range).

players.availability_reasoning: Minnesota traded significant draft capital to acquire Murray as their franchise QB solution. He is the unquestioned starter and the centerpiece of their offensive plans. The dead cap ($50M) makes a trade financially catastrophic. There is zero chance the Vikings move him unless the entire rebuild trajectory changes.
players.injury_notes: Tore ACL in December 2022, missed most of 2023 season. Returned to full health in 2024 and played 16 games. History of minor hamstring and ankle injuries throughout career. The ACL is the major red flag but appears fully behind him.
players.character_notes: Has faced periodic criticism for leadership style and preparation habits. Some reports out of Arizona suggested friction with coaching staff before the trade. Nothing suspendable or legally concerning — more 'culture fit' questions that appear resolved in Minnesota.

players.name: Tyrion Ingram-Dawkins
players.performance_evidence: Ingram-Dawkins was a 4th-round pick out of Georgia in 2024 and earned a rotational role as a rookie defensive end in Minnesota's scheme. He showed promise as a run defender with his 6'3, 290+ pound frame and flashed some interior pass-rush ability. As a rookie he logged roughly 300-400 defensive snaps in a rotational capacity, contributing 2-3 sacks and solid run-stop grades. He's projecting into a larger DE2 role for 2026 as the Vikings develop him, but he hasn't yet established himself as a full-time starter. Upside is real given his age and physical profile but production is still modest.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap rotational defensive end on a rookie 4th-round deal with 3 years of control. The contract and age provide upside value, but he hasn't produced enough to warrant significant draft capital. Worth a 5th-round pick to a team that sees developmental potential in his physical tools.

players.availability_reasoning: As a young rotational player still on his rookie deal, Ingram-Dawkins has some value but isn't a core piece Minnesota would refuse to move. If a team offered a 4th-round pick the Vikings would likely consider it, though they'd prefer to keep developing him given his upside and minimal cost. He's not being shopped but is the type of player who changes hands for the right price.

players.name: Isaiah Rodgers
players.performance_evidence: Rodgers has had one of the more unusual career arcs in the NFL. A 6th-round pick by Indianapolis in 2020, he showed promise as a return specialist and backup corner before receiving an indefinite suspension for gambling violations that cost him the entire 2023 season. He was reinstated and signed with Philadelphia for 2024, where he earned a starting role and played well — grading out as a solid outside corner with good ball skills and recovery speed. Minnesota signed him to start at CB1 in 2025, and he's proven to be a legitimate starting-caliber corner. Not a shutdown guy, but a reliable cover man who can handle WR2s and hold up against WR1s in a scheme with safety help. Roughly 12-14 PDs per season when starting full-time, with adequate tackler ability.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting corner on a very affordable deal ($5.5M AAV) with 2 years of control — that's well below market for a starting CB. The value-to-contract ratio is strong. However, his gambling suspension history creates some discount, and he's a solid starter rather than a true CB1 talent. Worth a 3rd-round pick given the contract and positional value of cornerback.

players.availability_reasoning: Rodgers is the CB1 on the depth chart and playing at a starting-caliber level on a team-friendly deal. Minnesota would need a strong offer (high 3rd or better) to consider moving their top corner. They're not shopping him, but the contract value means he'd be attractive to other teams, and the Vikings would listen to serious offers if they had depth behind him.
players.character_notes: Received indefinite suspension in 2023 for violating the NFL's gambling policy while with the Indianapolis Colts. Was reinstated for the 2024 season. This is a meaningful character flag — some teams will not trade for players with gambling violations. He has by all accounts been a model citizen since reinstatement.

players.name: Jay Ward
players.performance_evidence: Ward was a 4th-round selection out of LSU in the 2024 draft, valued for his versatility — he played corner, safety, and nickel in college. Minnesota has deployed him primarily at free safety where he earned starting snaps as a rookie and moved into the FS1 role. He has good range and instincts in coverage, flashing playmaking ability, but is still developing consistency in his reads and tackling. As a second-year player entering 2026, he profiles as a below-average to average starter who could develop into something more. His athletic profile (sub-4.45 speed, versatile coverage skills) gives him a solid floor.

trade_value.reasoning: Young safety starter on a dirt-cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. The contract and age are the main value drivers — safety is a low-positional-value spot, and he's only a below-average starter at this point. Worth a 4th-round pick, with the upside that he could develop into more given his athletic tools and youth.

players.availability_reasoning: Ward is the starting free safety on a cheap rookie contract with years of team control. The Vikings invested a 4th-round pick in him just two years ago and are counting on his development. They wouldn't move him cheaply, but safety isn't a premium position, and if someone offered a 3rd-round pick, they'd have to seriously consider it.
